rationale: dim1: Answer key group=control. Output misstatement_probability=30; control rule p<=30 -> 2. Score 2. dim2: Control case -> null per rubric; output correctly asserts no mechanism_hypotheses (empty list), consistent with p=30<40 so no hypothesis required. dim3: Control -> nulls (answer key genre_tag_row=null; no top-ranked hypothesis exists). dim4: Consistency rule check first: p=30 (<40) requires tier clear/watch; declared risk_tier='watch' -> no cap. Evidence is specific and concrete throughout: exact XBRL-style values with accession numbers (e.g., CL1: AR 40,550,000->53,402,000 vs revenue 359,449,000->394,288,000; CL3: OCF 34,015,000 vs NI 23,044,000 in 2011 and 46,938,000 vs 19,710,000 in 2012; CL6: allowance 593,000->464,000->892,000 across 2010-2012). The top_signals combine multiple years and multiple data points coherently — e.g., the AR/inventory growth flags are cross-referenced against the Q4 2012 acquisition (Goodwill 61,144,000->175,108,000; LongTermDebt 30,737,000->237,566,000) to explain divergence as purchase-accounting effects, and quarterly NI volatility (loss quarter in Q3 2010, Q4 2012 drop to 641,000) is used to reject smoothing. Any implicit mechanism assertions on this control case (acquisition-driven balance growth, routine 8-K/A pro forma amendments) are supported by cited provided data. This meets the '2 plus coherent multi-year/multi-point combination' bar -> 3. memorization_suspect_condition2: false — the reasoning points at concrete provided data content (specific balances, flows, dates) rather than merely listing documents, and mentions no post-cutoff facts (no revelation/enforcement/outcome references; latest cited data is Q1 2013 filing within the document set).
